game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster's Hidden Treasure

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed levels, find Gogo Dodo to unlock portal progression, defeat brainwashed friends and boss enemies to advance
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Rescue narrative (Babs Bunny) combined with combat-platforming challenges and optional treasure collection in bonus levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Buster Bunny to rescue Babs from Montana Max while battling iconic Tiny Toons villains in varied environments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy reliance on IP integration—brainwashed companions as progressive boss encounters, Wackyland bonus portals as risk/reward optional content. Level variety emphasized (woods, snow, different landscapes) as core design intent

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Optional Wackyland bonus levels provide risk/reward gameplay where players can attempt item collection before Dodo contact forces exit

progression.unlock_mechanism: Find Gogo Dodo in each level to access portal for level progression; defeat Gene Splicer to free brainwashed companions and advance narrative
replayability_hooks: Wackyland bonus levels with item collection scoring
Multiple distinct landscapes and themed environments
Brainwashed companion encounters as repeatable boss fights

world_building_techniques: IP-driven character narrative (Buster/Babs relationship, Montana Max as treasure thief)
Brainwashing subplot with Gene Splicer as antagonist
Wackyland portal system ties directly to show lore
Environmental variety (woods, snow) reflects show's aesthetic range

boss_design.philosophy: Narrative-driven boss encounters: brainwashed companions (Plucky Duck, Hamton J. Pig, Dizzy Devil, Calamity Coyote) serve as mid-game bosses; Gene Splicer as primary antagonist; Montana Max as final objective
notable_bosses: Gene Splicer (primary antagonist, helmet controlling friends falls off after defeat)
Brainwashed companions as sequential boss encounters

environmental_storytelling: Themed level environments (woods, snow) establish geographical narrative progression
Brainwashing visual indicators on companion characters
Wackyland bonus areas as distinct story location

sources_used: Wikipedia - Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster's Hidden Treasure (gameplay section, reception data)
